content.full_text:
February 2, 1977


The Honorable James K. Turner
Judge of the Superior Court
County of Orange
Santa Ana, California

Dear Judge Turner:

     This letter is in response to your request for advice
concerning whether or not income from a minor non-dependent
child is reportable on a Statement of Economic Interests.

     You have stated that the income received by the child
is derived from a trust set-up by the maternal grand-
parents of the child and that you may not directly or
indirectly benefit from the trust or the income. You are, there-
fore, not required to disclose the income on your Statement
of Economic Interests.

     Please let me know if I may be of additional assistance.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        Regina Siciliano Kutchins
                                        Technical Assistance Program
                                        Coordinator

RSK:av

Chamber of
JAMES K. TURNER
Judge of Superior Court

Superior Court of the State of California
County of Orange
Santa Ana, California
January 26, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
Attn: Dawn Wiser
Conflict of Interest Division
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95805

Gentlemen:

As a Judge of the Superior Court, I am required to file
a Statement of Economic Interests, Form 721, by March 1, 1977.
In order to properly complete this statement, I will appreciate
an answer to the following question.

I am the father of a minor child who does not reside
with me and for whom I am paying child support in the amount
of $250 per month. This sum does not amount to 50 percent
of her support and in addition she has her own income on
which she pays income taxes. I would like a determination as
to whether or not I am required to declare her income or
investments on the required Statement of Economic Interest.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request.

Very truly yours,

James K. Turner
Judge of the Superior Court

JKT:m
